decks.notes: This Aggro - Death and Taxes deck focuses on utilizing cheap and efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Galvanic Discharge to pressure the opponent while disrupting their game plan with cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Solitude. The deck also includes control elements like Consign to Memory and Prismatic Ending to deal with threats. The goal is to quickly outpace the opponent while disrupting their strategies, ultimately leading to a swift victory.

This deck focuses on cycling through creatures with abilities like drawing cards and countering spells until you can cast Living End to bring all those creatures back onto the battlefield at once. It includes cards that can manipulate the top of your deck, protect your combo pieces, and disrupt your opponent's strategy. The key is to control the game until you can unleash a powerful combo that overwhelms your opponent and secures your victory.

The deck focuses on a fast-paced aggressive strategy, utilizing powerful creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury to quickly overwhelm the opponent. Support cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Guide of Souls provide buffs and protection for your creatures, while cards like Goblin Bombardment and Ranger-Captain of Eos offer additional utility and removal options. The mana base is designed to support the aggressive game plan, with efficient land cards like Arid Mesa and Windswept Heath to ensure consistent mana access. The overall goal is to apply pressure early and keep the opponent on the back foot.

This Jeskai Control deck focuses on utilizing a variety of removal spells and disruption to control the game, while also using cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Teferi, Time Raveler to generate advantage and lock opponents out of certain strategies. The deck also includes a companion, Kaheera, the Orphanguard, to provide additional synergy with the creature-based spells in the deck. Ultimately, the goal of this deck is to outlast and outmaneuver opponents, using a combination of powerful planeswalkers and versatile removal spells to control the flow of the game.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with a combination of powerful creatures and burn spells. The key strategy is to quickly deploy threats like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury, while using burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to control the board and finish off the opponent. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Seasoned Pyromancer provide additional value and resilience to keep up the pressure. The deck also includes some utility lands and versatile spells to adapt to different matchups.

The deck focuses on utilizing the synergy between Basking Broodscale's ability to buff creatures with +1/+1 counters and Blade of the Bloodchief to create a powerful army of creatures. By casting Glaring Fleshraker and Devourer of Destiny, the player can trigger multiple instances of Basking Broodscale's ability in a single turn, overwhelming the opponent with a large number of buffed creatures. Additionally, cards like Urza's Saga and Kozilek's Command provide additional utility and support to the combo strategy, while Emrakul, the Promised End serves as a powerful finisher.

The main idea of this Dimir Control deck is to heavily disrupt your opponent's game plan by using a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage to control the pace of the game. By utilizing cards like Counterspell, Fatal Push, and Consult the Star Charts, you can prevent your opponent from developing their board while setting up your own win conditions. With a mix of disruption, card draw, and removal, this deck aims to outlast and outmaneuver your opponent until you can secure the win.

The deck focuses on quickly reanimating powerful creatures from the graveyard using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Ephemerate. The strategy involves using Atraxa, Grand Unifier to help control the board while setting up the combo. Leyline Binding helps protect key pieces while creatures like Iona, Shield of Emeria and Overlord of the Balemurk provide powerful effects when reanimated. Satyr Wayfinder and Raffine's Informant help dig for key pieces while cards like Solitude offer removal options. Overall, the deck aims to quickly reanimate threats and overwhelm the opponent.

The main idea behind this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan through powerful counter spells and removal spells while generating card advantage through draw spells like Consult the Star Charts and Wan Shi Tong, Librarian. The deck aims to control the board and outvalue the opponent by efficiently dealing with threats and controlling the pace of the game. It also utilizes graveyard interaction with cards like Cling to Dust to further disrupt the opponent's strategy. Overall, the deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of counters, removal, and card advantage.

The deck focuses on utilizing low-cost aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, along with burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Thraben Charm, to quickly deal damage to the opponent. It also incorporates cards like Goblin Bombardment and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah for additional sources of damage. With a mix of creatures, burn spells, and support cards like Ocelot Pride and Arena of Glory, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast and efficient aggression while maintaining board control.

The key concept of this deck is to maintain aggressive pressure on the opponent by utilizing cards that disrupt their game plan, while also deploying powerful threats like Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer. The deck aims to control the board through abilities like Galvanic Discharge and Solitude, while also utilizing cards like Teferi, Time Raveler to slow down the opponent's game plan. This deck is designed to apply pressure early and disrupt the opponent's strategy, allowing for a quick victory through a combination of aggression and control.

This deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly with cards like Arboreal Grazer and Amulet of Vigor to bring out powerful creatures like Primeval Titan and effectively combo with cards like Scapeshift and Urza's Saga to overwhelm opponents with massive creatures and combo chains. Utilizing lands like Gruul Turf and Simic Growth Chamber to bounce and reuse lands for maximum efficiency, this deck aims to quickly assemble its pieces for explosive plays and win conditions. The key strategy is to efficiently ramp mana, search for combo pieces, and overwhelm opponents with powerful creatures and combos.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. The key concept revolves around using efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to apply pressure, while also utilizing cards like Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury and Goblin Bombardment for added reach. The deck aims to close out games quickly by dealing damage directly to the opponent's life total, leveraging its powerful synergies and efficient mana base to maintain a relentless assault.

The deck focuses on utilizing sacrifice effects, graveyard recursion, and +1/+1 counters to create a powerful engine that overwhelms the opponent. By sacrificing creatures like Basking Broodscale and Delighted Halfling, along with using cards like Malevolent Rumble and Grist, the Hunger Tide, the deck can generate value and refill its board. Additionally, cards like Wall of Roots and Walking Ballista provide additional utility and ways to control the game. Boseiju, Who Endures and Agatha's Soul Cauldron provide protection and additional card advantage to help the deck combo off and secure victory.

Utilizing a mix of powerful Eldrazi creatures and reanimation spells, this deck aims to cheat large threats like Emrakul and Ulamog onto the battlefield quickly and unexpectedly. Through using cards like Through the Breach and Goryo's Vengeance, the deck can surprise opponents with explosive plays. The inclusion of Thoughtseize and Kozilek's Command helps to disrupt the opponent's game plan while also providing additional ways to get key creatures into the graveyard for reanimation. The deck's key strategy revolves around combining instant reanimation with powerful Eldrazi creatures to overwhelm opponents and secure victory.

The core strategy of this Modern Ruby Storm deck revolves around casting multiple cheap spells to generate mana with Ruby Medallion and mana rituals, while utilizing Ral, Monsoon Mage and Glimpse the Impossible to draw cards and dig for key combo pieces. The deck aims to storm off by chaining spells together to generate enough mana and spells to cast Past in Flames and Grapeshot for the win. The deck also includes some land and spell tutoring and card selection to help assemble the combo pieces quickly and efficiently.
